from maspy import *

class Room(Environment):
    def add_dirt(self, agent, position):
        self.print(f"Dirt created in position {position}")
        dirt_status = self.get(Percept("dirt",Any))
        dirt_status.values[position] = False # changes the dict inside percept
    
    def clean_position(self, agent, position):
        self.print(f"{agent} is cleaning position {position}")
        dirt_status = self.get(Percept("dirt",Any))
        if dirt_status.values[position] is False:
            dirt_status.values[position] = True # changes the dict inside percept

class Robot(Agent):
    def __init__(self, name, initial_env=None, full_log=False):
        super().__init__(name, show_exec=full_log)
        self.connect_to(initial_env)
        self.add(Goal("decide_move"))
        self.add(Belief("room_is_dirty"))
        self.position = (0,0)
        self.print(f"Inicial position {self.position}")

    @pl(gain,Goal("decide_move"))
    def decide_move(self,src):
        min_dist = float("inf")
        target = None
 
        dirt_pos = self.get(Belief("dirt",Any,"Room"))
        x, y = self.position
        for pos, clean in dirt_pos.values.items():
            if not clean:
                dist = abs(pos[0]-x) + abs(pos[1]-y)
                if dist < min_dist:
                    min_dist = dist
                    target = pos
        
        if target is None:
            self.print(f"All dirt is cleaned")
            self.rm(Belief("room_is_dirty"))

TITLE: Managing Beliefs and Goals

Here are some info about Beliefs and Goals being created and removed.

- This function adds a goal to the agent;
- The first field represents the goal key and must always be a string;
- The second field represents arguments of the goal and will always be a tuple;
- Each argument can have any structure, with each position of the tuple representing a different one;
- The third field represents the goal source. It is "self" by default, or another agent.
- adding or removing a goal always creates an event for agent, which will try to find a applicable plan.

```py
agent = DummyAgent("Ag")
agent.add( Goal(Name, Values, Source) )
agent.rm( Goal(Name, Values, Source) )
        
agent.add( Goal("check_house", {"Area": [50,100], "Rooms": 5}, ("Seller",27) ) )
agent.add( Goal("SendInfo", ("Information",["List","of","Information",42]) ) )
agent.rm( Goal("walk", source=("trainer",2)) )
```

- This function adds a belief to the agent;
- The first an second field work exaclty the same way as the goal's
- The third field represents the belief source. It is "self" by default, another agent or an environment.
- The fourth field dictates if the adding or removing the belief will generate a new event.
- By default it does, but sometimes one does not want a group of beliefs to be considerend new events

```py
agent = DummyAgent("Ag")
agent.add( Belief(Name, Values, Source, Adds_Event) )
agent.rm( Belief(Name, Values, Source, Adds_Event) )

agent.add( Belief("Dirt", (("remaining",3),[(2,2),(3,7),(5,1)])) )
agent.rm( Belief("spot",("A",4,"free"),"Parking",False) )
agent.add( Belief("velocity",57) )
```

TITLE: Defining Plans

To define plans it is also really simple, it only needs the @pl decoration. This decoration must contain the plan change {gain, lose or test}, the data that changed {Belief(s) or Goal(s)} and optionally a context needed to be true to execute the plan {Belief(s) or Goal(s)}.

```py
    change: TypeVar('gain'|'lose'|'test')
    changed_data: Iterable[Belief | Goal] | Belief | Goal
    context: Iterable[Belief | Goal] | Belief | Goal

    @pl(change, changed_data, context)
    def foo(self,src, *changed_data.values, *context.values):
```

Resuming the the driver example, you can implement a plan the following way:

```py
from maspy import *

class Driver(Agent):
    def __init__(self, agent_name=None):
        super().__init__(agent_name)
        self.add(Belief("budget",(rnd.randint(6,10),rnd.randint(12,20)),adds_event=False))
        self.add(Goal("park"))

    # This plan will be executed whenever the agent gains the goal "checkPrice"
    # Every plan needs at least self and src, plus the arguments from the trigger and context
    # for this plan, the context is the belief of a budget with wanted and max prices
    @pl(gain,Goal("checkPrice", Any),Belief("budget",(Any, Any)))
    def check_price(self, src, given_price, budget):
        ...

driver = Driver("Drv")
```

TITLE: Environment

MASPY also gives an abstraction to model the environment.

Here's how you create a parking lot for the manager and driver from before:

Creating an environment

```py
from maspy import *

class Park(Environment):
    def __init__(self, env_name=None):
        super().__init__(env_name)
        # This creates in the environment a percept for connected agents to perceive.
        # This specific percept does not create a event when percieved by an agent 
        self.create(Percept("spot",(1,"free"),adds_event=False))

    def park_spot(self, agt, spot_id):
        # The function get gives you percepts from the environment
        # It has various filters to make this search more precise
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",(spot_id,"free")))
        if spot:
            # This function is used to modify the arguments of an percept.
            self.change(spot,(spot_id,driver))

            # You could also remove the old and create the new
            self.create(Percept("spot",(spot_id,driver)))

    def leave_spot(self, agt):
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",("ID",driver)))
        if spot:
            self.change(spot,(spot.values[0],"free"))
```

from maspy import *
from random import choice

class Cross_Manager(Agent):
    @pl(gain, Belief("traffic_light", Any, "Crossing"))
    def traffic_light(self, src, color):
        vehicles = self.list_agents("Vehicle","Env","Cross_Junction")
        for vehicle in vehicles:
            self.print(f"Detected traffic light: {color} in env {src} - sending signal to {vehicle}")
            self.send(vehicle, achieve, Goal("crossing_over"), "Crossing")
    
    @pl(gain, Belief("leaving_junction"))
    def end_watch(self,src):
        self.stop_cycle()

class Vehicle(Agent):
    @pl(gain, Goal("crossing_over"))
    def crossing(self,src):
        self.print(f"Confirmation for crossing by {src}")
        self.cross()
        self.print("Crossing Completed")
        self.send(src, tell, Belief("leaving_junction"), "Crossing")
        self.stop_cycle()

class Crossing(Environment):
    def __init__(self, env_name):
        super().__init__(env_name)
        self.colors = ["red", "yellow", "green"]
        self.create(Percept("traffic_light", choice(self.colors)))
    
    def cross(self, agt):
        self.print(f"Agent {agt} is now crossing")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    cross_channel = Channel("Crossing")
    cross_env = Crossing("Cross_Junction")
    agent_list: list = [Cross_Manager("CrossManager")]
    for _ in range(3):
        agent_list.append(Vehicle())
    Admin().report = True

TITLE: Managing Beliefs and Goals

Here are some info about Beliefs and Goals being created and removed.

- This function adds a goal to the agent;
- The first field represents the goal key and must always be a string;
- The second field represents arguments of the goal and will always be a tuple;
- Each argument can have any structure, with each position of the tuple representing a different one;
- The third field represents the goal source. It is "self" by default, or another agent.
- adding or removing a goal always creates an event for agent, which will try to find a applicable plan.

```py
agent = DummyAgent("Ag")
agent.add( Goal(Name, Values, Source) )
agent.rm( Goal(Name, Values, Source) )
        
agent.add( Goal("check_house", {"Area": [50,100], "Rooms": 5}, ("Seller",27) ) )
agent.add( Goal("SendInfo", ("Information",["List","of","Information",42]) ) )
agent.rm( Goal("walk", source=("trainer",2)) )
```

- This function adds a belief to the agent;
- The first an second field work exaclty the same way as the goal's
- The third field represents the belief source. It is "self" by default, another agent or an environment.
- The fourth field dictates if the adding or removing the belief will generate a new event.
- By default it does, but sometimes one does not want a group of beliefs to be considerend new events

```py
agent = DummyAgent("Ag")
agent.add( Belief(Name, Values, Source, Adds_Event) )
agent.rm( Belief(Name, Values, Source, Adds_Event) )

agent.add( Belief("Dirt", (("remaining",3),[(2,2),(3,7),(5,1)])) )
agent.rm( Belief("spot",("A",4,"free"),"Parking",False) )
agent.add( Belief("velocity",57) )
```

TITLE: Defining Plans

To define plans it is also really simple, it only needs the @pl decoration. This decoration must contain the plan change {gain, lose or test}, the data that changed {Belief(s) or Goal(s)} and optionally a context needed to be true to execute the plan {Belief(s) or Goal(s)}.

```py
    change: TypeVar('gain'|'lose'|'test')
    changed_data: Iterable[Belief | Goal] | Belief | Goal
    context: Iterable[Belief | Goal] | Belief | Goal

    @pl(change, changed_data, context)
    def foo(self,src, *changed_data.values, *context.values):
```

Resuming the the driver example, you can implement a plan the following way:

```py
from maspy import *

class Driver(Agent):
    def __init__(self, agent_name=None):
        super().__init__(agent_name)
        self.add(Belief("budget",(rnd.randint(6,10),rnd.randint(12,20)),adds_event=False))
        self.add(Goal("park"))

    # This plan will be executed whenever the agent gains the goal "checkPrice"
    # Every plan needs at least self and src, plus the arguments from the trigger and context
    # for this plan, the context is the belief of a budget with wanted and max prices
    @pl(gain,Goal("checkPrice", Any),Belief("budget",(Any, Any)))
    def check_price(self, src, given_price, budget):
        ...

driver = Driver("Drv")
```

TITLE: Environment

MASPY also gives an abstraction to model the environment.

Here's how you create a parking lot for the manager and driver from before:

Creating an environment

```py
from maspy import *

class Park(Environment):
    def __init__(self, env_name=None):
        super().__init__(env_name)
        # This creates in the environment a percept for connected agents to perceive.
        # This specific percept does not create a event when percieved by an agent 
        self.create(Percept("spot",(1,"free"),adds_event=False))

    def park_spot(self, agt, spot_id):
        # The function get gives you percepts from the environment
        # It has various filters to make this search more precise
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",(spot_id,"free")))
        if spot:
            # This function is used to modify the arguments of an percept.
            self.change(spot,(spot_id,driver))

            # You could also remove the old and create the new
            self.create(Percept("spot",(spot_id,driver)))

    def leave_spot(self, agt):
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",("ID",driver)))
        if spot:
            self.change(spot,(spot.values[0],"free"))
```

Allowing agents to interact with an environment

from maspy import *
import random as rnd
from time import sleep

class Parking(Environment):
    def __init__(self, env_name,nr_spots=10):
        super().__init__(env_name)
        self.create(Percept("sold_spots",0,"Spots",False))
        self.print(f"Starting parking with {nr_spots} spots")
        for n in range(1,nr_spots+1):
            self.create(Percept("spot",(n,"free"),"Spots",False))
    
    def park_spot(self, agt, spot_id):
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",(spot_id,"free")))
        self.print(f"Driver {agt} parking on spot({spot_id})")
        if spot:
            self.change(spot,(spot_id,[agt]))
            return True
        else:
            self.print(f"Requested spot({spot_id}) unavailable")
            return None
    
    def leave_spot(self, agt):
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",(Any,[agt])))
        if spot:
            self.print(f"Driver {agt} leaving spot({spot.values[0]})")
            self.change(spot,(spot.values[0],"free"))
        else:
            self.print(f"Driver {agt} not found in any spot")

class Manager(Agent):
    def __init__(self, agt_name=None):
        super().__init__(agt_name,read_all_mail=True)
        self.add(Belief("spotPrice",15,adds_event=False))
        self.add(Belief("minPrice",10,adds_event=False))
        self.add(Goal("broadcast_price"))
        self.print("Manager initialized")
        self.end_counter = 0
    
    @pl(gain,Goal("broadcast_price"), Belief("spotPrice",Any))

from maspy import *

class Room(Environment):
    def add_dirt(self, agent, position):
        self.print(f"Dirt created in position {position}")
        dirt_status = self.get(Percept("dirt",Any))
        dirt_status.values[position] = False # changes the dict inside percept
    
    def clean_position(self, agent, position):
        self.print(f"{agent} is cleaning position {position}")
        dirt_status = self.get(Percept("dirt",Any))
        if dirt_status.values[position] is False:
            dirt_status.values[position] = True # changes the dict inside percept

class Robot(Agent):
    def __init__(self, name, initial_env=None, full_log=False):
        super().__init__(name, show_exec=full_log)
        self.connect_to(initial_env)
        self.add(Goal("decide_move"))
        self.add(Belief("room_is_dirty"))
        self.position = (0,0)
        self.print(f"Inicial position {self.position}")

    @pl(gain,Goal("decide_move"))
    def decide_move(self,src):
        min_dist = float("inf")
        target = None
 
        dirt_pos = self.get(Belief("dirt",Any,"Room"))
        x, y = self.position
        for pos, clean in dirt_pos.values.items():
            if not clean:
                dist = abs(pos[0]-x) + abs(pos[1]-y)
                if dist < min_dist:
                    min_dist = dist
                    target = pos
        
        if target is None:
            self.print(f"All dirt is cleaned")
            self.rm(Belief("room_is_dirty"))

Generate one complete MASPY Python program from the task description.

        OUTPUT
        - ONLY Python code
        - First line: from maspy import *
        - No comments, prose, markdown, or extra text
        - Include a normal Python entrypoint using if __name__ == "__main__":
        - Only output FAIL if the task is fundamentally impossible or self-contradictory under the MASPY subset below
        - If a previous attempt failed, repair it instead of outputting FAIL

        CORE
        - Use the MASPY subset shown in REFERENCE CONTEXT
        - Do NOT invent APIs, helpers, or shortcuts
        - Follow BDI: goals/beliefs trigger plans
        - Favor the smallest runnable implementation that satisfies the task

        PLANS
        - Only inside Agent classes
        - Only use: @pl(gain, ...)
        - Allowed in @pl: Goal(...), Belief(...) ONLY
        - NEVER use Percept(...) in @pl
        - NO keyword arguments in @pl
        - Plan body may call Environment helper methods through self.env
        - MASPY plan decorator shape is: @pl(change, changed_data, context)
        - After gain, use one trigger term and at most one context argument
        - If more than one context condition is needed, wrap them in a list as the third argument
        - Never write a fourth positional argument in @pl(...); MASPY will ignore it and the plan signature will break
        - If a plan is intended to run repeatedly while values change over time, never hardcode those changing values in Goal(...) or Belief(...) inside the decorator
        - For changing Goal/Belief values, use wildcard matching with Any, for example Goal("collect", (Any,)) and Belief("coin_count", (Any,))
        - Any is allowed in Goal(...) and Belief(...), but never in Percept(...)

        SIGNATURE RULE (CRITICAL)
        - Plan must be: def name(self, src, ...)
        - Include ONE parameter per value contained in changed_data and context Goal/Belief terms
        - Goal values come before Belief context values
        - Goal/Belief terms with no values contribute no parameter
        - If context is a list, count values across every item in the list

        Examples:
        @pl(gain, Goal("turn_on"), Belief("light_state", ("off",)))
        → def turn_light_on(self, src, light_state)

        @pl(gain, Goal("collect", (3,)), Belief("coin", (1,)))
        → def collect_coin(self, src, target, coin_value)

        @pl(gain, Goal("patrol"), [Belief("patrol_point", (Any,)), Belief("alert_state", ("pending",))])
        → def patrol(self, src, patrol_point, alert_state)

        - NEVER mismatch parameters

        PERCEPTS
        - Environment owns percept creation and percept mutation
        - Inside Environment classes, self.create(...), self.add(...), self.rm(...), self.get(...), and self.change(...) must operate on Percept(...), not Belief(...) or Goal(...)
        - Agents should not create or change Percept objects directly
        - NEVER use Any
        - NEVER use values_tuple=
        - Do NOT use True or False inside Percept values; encode state with strings instead

        BELIEFS
        - Use add(...) and rm(...)
        - Remove old belief before adding updated one
        - Use Python variables for counters
        - If a looping goal still has work left, re-add that goal
        - If the current looping goal has finished, stop, terminate, or return without calling self.rm(Goal(...)) on the same trigger goal
        - If the task says to stop or terminate after success, the success branch must explicitly call self.stop_cycle()
        - Do NOT rely on a goal being cleared automatically to stop the program
        - Do NOT use True or False inside Belief or Goal values; encode state with strings like "pending", "sent", "locked", "unlocked", "detected"

        FORBIDDEN
        get_percepts, self.get_percepts, update_belief, get_belief,
        set_belief, delete_belief, has_belief,
        self.world, world., Admin().get_environment(),
        self.update(, self.delete(, delete(,
        wait(, sleep(, log(, self.change(

        RUNTIME
        - Exactly ONE Environment class
        - At least ONE Agent class
        - Instantiate the environment in the entrypoint
        - Instantiate each agent with the environment and store it as self.env
        - Use Admin().connect_to([...agents...], env) before Admin().start_system()

        CONSTRAINTS
        - Do NOT scan percepts unless shown in CONTEXT
        - Do NOT invent environment APIs
        - Use ONLY demonstrated patterns
        - Keep implementation minimal and correct
        - NO extra features, logging, randomness

        IMPLEMENTATION PATTERN
        - Put task state owned by the world into the Environment as plain Python attributes.
        - Expose one small Environment method for each state-changing action the agent must perform.
        - Let plans trigger from Goal(...) and Belief(...) updates, not percept decorators.
        - For counter tasks: read the current belief value, call the environment helper, rm the old belief, add the new belief, then either re-add the goal or stop.
        - Re-add a goal only when more work is still required.
        - When a plan updates a belief that appears in its decorator context, that decorator context should usually use Any rather than a hardcoded literal.
        - When the final success state is reached, call self.stop_cycle() in that success path so the run finishes before timeout.
        - Represent boolean-like state symbolically using strings, not Python booleans inside MASPY terms.

        CRITICAL SIGNATURE LAW
        - MASPY uses @pl(change, changed_data, context).
        - The plan function must be:
        def plan_name(self, src, <changed_data values>, <context values>)
        - If there are multiple context conditions, they must be provided inside a list in the third decorator argument.
        - Every value inside the trigger and context terms contributes one positional argument.
        - If a Goal or Belief has no values, it contributes no extra argument.

        TASK
        - Environment name: DeliveryWorld
- Agent name: Courier
- One Courier agent
- Target: deliver 2 packages
- Initial belief: Belief("delivered_count", (0,))
- Initial belief: Belief("at_location", ("depot",))
- Initial goal: Goal("deliver_packages", (2,))
- The environment manages package locations and delivery destinations
- The agent must pick up one package from the depot
- The agent must travel to a delivery location
- The agent must deliver the package
- After each successful delivery:
- the agent gains Belief("delivered_package", (package_id,))
- the old delivered_count belief is removed
- the new delivered_count belief is added with the incremented value
- the agent returns to the depot if more packages remain
- If delivered_count is less than 2, re-add the deliver_packages goal
- Stop only after 2 packages have been delivered

- [stop_condition] Task requires an explicit stopping condition. The success path must call self.stop_cycle(); do not rely on goal clearance alone to end execution.

        ```python
class Courier(Agent):
    def __init__(self, agent_name=None):
        super().__init__(agent_name)
        self.add(Belief("at_location", "depot", adds_event=False))
        self.add(Goal("deliver_package"))

    @pl(gain, Goal("deliver_package"))
    def deliver_package(self, src):
        if self.get(Belief("at_location", "depot")):
            self.add(Belief("at_location", "delivery_point", adds_event=False))
            self.rm(Goal("deliver_package"))
            self.add(Goal("return_to_depot"))

    @pl(gain, Goal("return_to_depot"))
    def return_to_depot(self, src):
        if self.get(Belief("at_location", "delivery_point")):
            self.add(Belief("at_location", "depot", adds_event=False))
            self.rm(Goal("return_to_depot"))
            self.add(Goal("deliver_package"))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    courier = Courier("Courier")
    Admin().start_system()
        ```

```py
from maspy import *

class Park(Environment):
    def __init__(self, env_name=None):
        super().__init__(env_name)
        # This creates in the environment a percept for connected agents to perceive.
        # This specific percept does not create a event when percieved by an agent 
        self.create(Percept("spot",(1,"free"),adds_event=False))

    def park_spot(self, agt, spot_id):
        # The function get gives you percepts from the environment
        # It has various filters to make this search more precise
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",(spot_id,"free")))
```

from maspy import *
import random as rnd
from time import sleep

class Parking(Environment):
    def __init__(self, env_name,nr_spots=10):
        super().__init__(env_name)
        self.create(Percept("sold_spots",0,"Spots",False))
        self.print(f"Starting parking with {nr_spots} spots")
        for n in range(1,nr_spots+1):
            self.create(Percept("spot",(n,"free"),"Spots",False))
    
    def park_spot(self, agt, spot_id):
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",(spot_id,"free")))
        self.print(f"Driver {agt} parking on spot({spot_id})")
        if spot:
            self.change(spot,(spot_id,[agt]))
            return True
        else:
            self.print(f"Requested spot({spot_id}) unavailable")
            return None
    
    def leave_spot(self, agt):
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",(Any,[agt])))
        if spot:
            self.print(f"Driver {agt} leaving spot({spot.values[0]})")
            self.change(spot,(spot.values[0],"free"))
        else:
            self.print(f"Driver {agt} not found in any spot")

class Manager(Agent):
    def __init__(self, agt_name=None):
        super().__init__(agt_name,read_all_mail=True)
        self.add(Belief("spotPrice",15,adds_event=False))
        self.add(Belief("minPrice",10,adds_event=False))
        self.add(Goal("broadcast_price"))
        self.print("Manager initialized")
        self.end_counter = 0

TITLE: Managing Beliefs and Goals

Here are some info about Beliefs and Goals being created and removed.

- This function adds a goal to the agent;
- The first field represents the goal key and must always be a string;
- The second field represents arguments of the goal and will always be a tuple;
- Each argument can have any structure, with each position of the tuple representing a different one;
- The third field represents the goal source. It is "self" by default, or another agent.
- adding or removing a goal always creates an event for agent, which will try to find a applicable plan.

```py
agent = DummyAgent("Ag")
agent.add( Goal(Name, Values, Source) )
agent.rm( Goal(Name, Values, Source) )
        
agent.add( Goal("check_house", {"Area": [50,100], "Rooms": 5}, ("Seller",27) ) )
agent.add( Goal("SendInfo", ("Information",["List","of","Information",42]) ) )
agent.rm( Goal("walk", source=("trainer",2)) )
```

- This function adds a belief to the agent;
- The first an second field work exaclty the same way as the goal's
- The third field represents the belief source. It is "self" by default, another agent or an environment.
- The fourth field dictates if the adding or removing the belief will generate a new event.
- By default it does, but sometimes one does not want a group of beliefs to be considerend new events

```py
agent = DummyAgent("Ag")
agent.add( Belief(Name, Values, Source, Adds_Event) )
agent.rm( Belief(Name, Values, Source, Adds_Event) )

agent.add( Belief("Dirt", (("remaining",3),[(2,2),(3,7),(5,1)])) )
agent.rm( Belief("spot",("A",4,"free"),"Parking",False) )
agent.add( Belief("velocity",57) )
```

TITLE: Defining Plans

To define plans it is also really simple, it only needs the @pl decoration. This decoration must contain the plan change {gain, lose or test}, the data that changed {Belief(s) or Goal(s)} and optionally a context needed to be true to execute the plan {Belief(s) or Goal(s)}.

```py
    change: TypeVar('gain'|'lose'|'test')
    changed_data: Iterable[Belief | Goal] | Belief | Goal
    context: Iterable[Belief | Goal] | Belief | Goal

    @pl(change, changed_data, context)
    def foo(self,src, *changed_data.values, *context.values):
```

Resuming the the driver example, you can implement a plan the following way:

```py
from maspy import *

class Driver(Agent):
    def __init__(self, agent_name=None):
        super().__init__(agent_name)
        self.add(Belief("budget",(rnd.randint(6,10),rnd.randint(12,20)),adds_event=False))
        self.add(Goal("park"))

    # This plan will be executed whenever the agent gains the goal "checkPrice"
    # Every plan needs at least self and src, plus the arguments from the trigger and context
    # for this plan, the context is the belief of a budget with wanted and max prices
    @pl(gain,Goal("checkPrice", Any),Belief("budget",(Any, Any)))
    def check_price(self, src, given_price, budget):
        ...

driver = Driver("Drv")
```

TITLE: Environment

MASPY also gives an abstraction to model the environment.

Here's how you create a parking lot for the manager and driver from before:

Creating an environment

```py
from maspy import *

class Park(Environment):
    def __init__(self, env_name=None):
        super().__init__(env_name)
        # This creates in the environment a percept for connected agents to perceive.
        # This specific percept does not create a event when percieved by an agent 
        self.create(Percept("spot",(1,"free"),adds_event=False))

    def park_spot(self, agt, spot_id):
        # The function get gives you percepts from the environment
        # It has various filters to make this search more precise
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",(spot_id,"free")))
        if spot:
            # This function is used to modify the arguments of an percept.
            self.change(spot,(spot_id,driver))

            # You could also remove the old and create the new
            self.create(Percept("spot",(spot_id,driver)))

    def leave_spot(self, agt):
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",("ID",driver)))
        if spot:
            self.change(spot,(spot.values[0],"free"))
```

        ```python
        trip = self.env.get(Percept("trip"))
        if trip and trip.values[1] <= self.beliefs["budget"][1]:
            self.send(trip.values[0], achieve, Goal("trip_bought", trip.values[1]))
        else:
            self.send(trip.values[0], achieve, Goal("improve_trip"))

class Seller(Agent):
    def __init__(self, agt_name=None):
        super().__init__(agt_name)
        self.add(Belief("budget", (500, 1500), adds_event=False))
        self.add(Goal("announce"))

    @pl(gain, Goal("announce"))
    def create_trips(self, src):
        for _ in range(5):
            stops = randint(0, 6)
            price = randint(int(700 / (stops + 1)), 1000)
            trip = (stops, price)
            self.env.announce_trip(trip)

    @pl(gain, Goal("trip_bought"), Belief("budget", (Any, Any)))
    def sell_trip(self, src, trip_price):
        if trip_price <= self.beliefs["budget"][1]:
            self.print(f"Selling trip for {trip_price}")
            self.env.delist_trip(trip_price)
            self.send(src, tell, Belief("travel_ticket", trip_price))
        else:
            self.send(src, achieve, Goal("improve_trip"))

class Environment(Environment):
    def announce_trip(self, trip):
        self.create(Percept("trip", trip))

    def delist_trip(self, trip):
        trip_perc = self.get(Percept("trip", trip))
        self.delete(trip_perc)

if __name__ == "__main__":
    env = Environment()
    buyer = Buyer("Buyer")
    seller = Seller("Seller")
    Admin().connect_to([buyer, seller], env)
    Admin().start_system()
        ```

Here are some info about Beliefs and Goals being created and removed.

- This function adds a goal to the agent;
- The first field represents the goal key and must always be a string;
- The second field represents arguments of the goal and will always be a tuple;
- Each argument can have any structure, with each position of the tuple representing a different one;
- The third field represents the goal source. It is "self" by default, or another agent.
- adding or removing a goal always creates an event for agent, which will try to find a applicable plan.

```py
agent = DummyAgent("Ag")
agent.add( Goal(Name, Values, Source) )
agent.rm( Goal(Name, Values, Source) )
        
agent.add( Goal("check_house", {"Area": [50,100], "Rooms": 5}, ("Seller",27) ) )
agent.add( Goal("SendInfo", ("Information",["List","of","Information",42]) ) )
agent.rm( Goal("walk", source=("trainer",2)) )
```

- This function adds a belief to the agent;
- The first an second field work exaclty the same way as the goal's
- The third field represents the belief source. It is "self" by default, another agent or an environment.
- The fourth field dictates if the adding or removing the belief will generate a new event.
- By default it does, but sometimes one does not want a group of beliefs to be considerend new events

```py
agent = DummyAgent("Ag")
```

TITLE: Environment

MASPY also gives an abstraction to model the environment.

Here's how you create a parking lot for the manager and driver from before:

Creating an environment

```py
from maspy import *

class Park(Environment):
    def __init__(self, env_name=None):
        super().__init__(env_name)
        # This creates in the environment a percept for connected agents to perceive.
        # This specific percept does not create a event when percieved by an agent 
        self.create(Percept("spot",(1,"free"),adds_event=False))

    def park_spot(self, agt, spot_id):
        # The function get gives you percepts from the environment
        # It has various filters to make this search more precise
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",(spot_id,"free")))
        if spot:
            # This function is used to modify the arguments of an percept.
            self.change(spot,(spot_id,driver))

            # You could also remove the old and create the new
            self.create(Percept("spot",(spot_id,driver)))

    def leave_spot(self, agt):
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",("ID",driver)))
        if spot:
            self.change(spot,(spot.values[0],"free"))
```

TITLE: Communication Between Agents

After starting the agents they may use the default channel or be connected to private one.

```py
from maspy import *

class Manager(Agent):
    def __init__(self, agt_name=None):
        super().__init__(agt_name,show_exec=False,show_cycle=False)
        self.add(Belief("spotPrice",rnd.randint(12,20),adds_event=False))

    @pl(gain,Goal("sendPrice"),Belief("spotPrice", Any))
    def send_price(self,src,spot_price):
        # The agent manager sends a goal to the manager via the Parking channel
        self.send(src,achieve,Goal("checkPrice",spot_price),"Parking")

class Driver(Agent):
    def __init__(self, agt_name=None):
        super().__init__(agt_name,show_exec=False,show_cycle=False)
        self.add(Belief("budget",(rnd.randint(6,10),rnd.randint(12,20)),adds_event=False))
        self.add(Goal("park"))
    
    @pl(gain,Goal("park"))
    def ask_price(self,src):
        # The agent driver sends a goal to the manager via the Parking channel
        self.send("Manager", achieve, Goal("sendPrice"),"Parking")

park_ch = Channel("Parking")
manager = Manager()
driver = Driver("Drv")
Admin().connect_to([manager,driver],park_ch)
```

        - Do NOT invent APIs, helpers, or shortcuts

        PLANS
        - Only inside Agent classes
        - Only use: @pl(gain, ...)
        - Allowed in @pl: Goal(...), Belief(...) ONLY
        - NEVER use Percept(...) in @pl
        - NO keyword arguments in @pl
        - Plan body may call Environment helper methods through self.env
        - MASPY plan decorator shape is: @pl(change, changed_data, context)
        - After gain, use one trigger term and at most one context argument
        - If more than one context condition is needed, wrap them in a list as the third argument
        - Never write a fourth positional argument in @pl(...); MASPY will ignore it and the plan signature will break
        - If a plan is intended to run repeatedly while values change over time, never hardcode those changing values in Goal(...) or Belief(...) inside the decorator
        - For changing Goal/Belief values, use wildcard matching with Any, for example Goal("collect", (Any,)) and Belief("coin_count", (Any,))
        - Any is allowed in Goal(...) and Belief(...), but never in Percept(...)

        SIGNATURE RULE (CRITICAL)
        - Plan must be: def name(self, src, ...)
        - Include ONE parameter per value contained in changed_data and context Goal/Belief terms
        - Goal values come before Belief context values
        - Goal/Belief terms with no values contribute no parameter
        - If context is a list, count values across every item in the list

        Examples:
        @pl(gain, Goal("turn_on"), Belief("light_state", ("off",)))
        → def turn_light_on(self, src, light_state)

        @pl(gain, Goal("collect", (3,)), Belief("coin", (1,)))
        → def collect_coin(self, src, target, coin_value)

        @pl(gain, Goal("patrol"), [Belief("patrol_point", (Any,)), Belief("alert_state", ("pending",))])
        → def patrol(self, src, patrol_point, alert_state)

        - NEVER mismatch parameters

        PERCEPTS
        - Environment owns percept creation and percept mutation
        - Inside Environment classes, self.create(...), self.add(...), self.rm(...), self.get(...), and self.change(...) must operate on Percept(...), not Belief(...) or Goal(...)
        - Agents should not create or change Percept objects directly
        - NEVER use Any
        - NEVER use values_tuple=
        - Do NOT use True or False inside Percept values; encode state with strings instead

        BELIEFS
        - Use add(...) and rm(...)
        - Remove old belief before adding updated one
        - Use Python variables for counters
        - If a looping goal still has work left, re-add that goal
        - If the current looping goal has finished, stop, terminate, or return without calling self.rm(Goal(...)) on the same trigger goal
        - If the task says to stop or terminate after success, the success branch must explicitly call self.stop_cycle()
        - Do NOT rely on a goal being cleared automatically to stop the program
        - Do NOT use True or False inside Belief or Goal values; encode state with strings like "pending", "sent", "locked", "unlocked", "detected"

        set_belief, delete_belief, has_belief,
        self.world, world., Admin().get_environment(),
        self.update(, self.delete(, delete(,
        wait(, sleep(, log(, self.change(

        RUNTIME
        - Exactly ONE Environment class
        - At least ONE Agent class
        - Instantiate the environment in the entrypoint
        - Instantiate each agent with the environment and store it as self.env
        - Use Admin().connect_to([...agents...], env) before Admin().start_system()

        CONSTRAINTS
        - Do NOT scan percepts unless shown in CONTEXT
        - Do NOT invent environment APIs
        - Use ONLY demonstrated patterns
        - Keep implementation minimal and correct
        - NO extra features, logging, randomness

        IMPLEMENTATION PATTERN
        - Put task state owned by the world into the Environment as plain Python attributes.
        - Expose one small Environment method for each state-changing action the agent must perform.
        - Let plans trigger from Goal(...) and Belief(...) updates, not percept decorators.
        - For counter tasks: read the current belief value, call the environment helper, rm the old belief, add the new belief, then either re-add the goal or stop.
        - Re-add a goal only when more work is still required.
        - When a plan updates a belief that appears in its decorator context, that decorator context should usually use Any rather than a hardcoded literal.
        - When the final success state is reached, call self.stop_cycle() in that success path so the run finishes before timeout.
        - Represent boolean-like state symbolically using strings, not Python booleans inside MASPY terms.

        - MASPY uses @pl(change, changed_data, context).

        def plan_name(self, src, <changed_data values>, <context values>)
        - If there are multiple context conditions, they must be provided inside a list in the third decorator argument.
        - Every value inside the trigger and context terms contributes one positional argument.
        - If a Goal or Belief has no values, it contributes no extra argument.

        ```python
        from maspy import *

class Parking(Environment):
    def __init__(self, env_name, nr_spots=10):
        super().__init__(env_name)
        self.create(Percept("sold_spots", 0, "Spots", False))
        self.print(f"Starting parking with {nr_spots} spots")
        for n in range(1, nr_spots + 1):
            self.create(Percept("spot", (n, "free"), "Spots", False))

    def park_spot(self, agt, spot_id):
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot", (spot_id, "free")))
        self.print(f"Driver {agt} parking on spot({spot_id})")
        if spot:
            self.change(spot, (spot_id, [agt]))
            return True
        else:
            self.print(f"Requested spot({spot_id}) unavailable")
            return None

    def leave_spot(self, agt):
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot", (Any, [agt])))
        if spot:
            self.print(f"Driver {agt} leaving spot({spot.values[0]})")
            self.change(spot, (spot.values[0], "free"))
        else:
            self.print(f"Driver {agt} not found in any spot")

class Manager(Agent):
    def __init__(self, agt_name=None):
        super().__init__(agt_name, read_all_mail=True)
        self.add(Belief("spotPrice", 15, adds_event=False))
        self.add(Belief("minPrice", 10, adds_event=False))
        self.add(Goal("broadcast_price"))
        self.print("Manager initialized")
        self.end_counter = 0

    @pl(gain, Goal("broadcast_price"), Belief("spotPrice", Any))
    def broadcast_price(self, src, spot_price):
        self.print(f"Broadcasting price: {spot_price}")
        for driver in self.get_agents():
            driver.send(src, tell, Goal("checkPrice", {"price": spot_price}))

class Driver(Agent):
    def __init__(self, agt_name=None):
        super().__init__(agt_name)
        self.add(Belief("budget", (rnd.randint(6, 10), rnd.randint(12, 20)), adds_event=False))
        self.add(Goal("park"))
        self.add(Goal("checkPrice"))

    @pl(gain, Goal("checkPrice"), Belief("budget", Any))
    def check_price(self, src, given_price, budget):
        if given_price <= budget[1]:
            self.send(src, achieve, Goal("park", {"spot_id": 1}))
        else:
            self.send(src, achieve, Goal("improve", {"price": given_price}))

    @pl(gain, Goal("park"), Belief("budget", Any))
    def park(self, src, spot_id, budget):
        if self.env.park_spot(self, spot_id):
            self.print(f"Parked on spot {spot_id}")
        else:
            self.send(src, achieve, Goal("checkPrice"))
        ```

class Parking(Environment):

SOURCE: maspy_wiki

TITLE: Managing Beliefs and Goals

Here are some info about Beliefs and Goals being created and removed.

- This function adds a goal to the agent;
- The first field represents the goal key and must always be a string;
- The second field represents arguments of the goal and will always be a tuple;
- Each argument can have any structure, with each position of the tuple representing a different one;
- The third field represents the goal source. It is "self" by default, or another agent.
- adding or removing a goal always creates an event for agent, which will try to find a applicable plan.

```py
agent = DummyAgent("Ag")
agent.add( Goal(Name, Values, Source) )
agent.rm( Goal(Name, Values, Source) )
        
agent.add( Goal("check_house", {"Area": [50,100], "Rooms": 5}, ("Seller",27) ) )
agent.add( Goal("SendInfo", ("Information",["List","of","Information",42]) ) )
agent.rm( Goal("walk", source=("trainer",2)) )
```

- This function adds a belief to the agent;
- The first an second field work exaclty the same way as the goal's
- The third field represents the belief source. It is "self" by default, another agent or an environment.
- The fourth field dictates if the adding or removing the belief will generate a new event.
- By default it does, but sometimes one does not want a group of beliefs to be considerend new events

```py
agent = DummyAgent("Ag")
agent.add( Belief(Name, Values, Source, Adds_Event) )
agent.rm( Belief(Name, Values, Source, Adds_Event) )

agent.add( Belief("Dirt", (("remaining",3),[(2,2),(3,7),(5,1)])) )
agent.rm( Belief("spot",("A",4,"free"),"Parking",False) )
agent.add( Belief("velocity",57) )
```

TITLE: Defining Plans

To define plans it is also really simple, it only needs the @pl decoration. This decoration must contain the plan change {gain, lose or test}, the data that changed {Belief(s) or Goal(s)} and optionally a context needed to be true to execute the plan {Belief(s) or Goal(s)}.

```py
    change: TypeVar('gain'|'lose'|'test')
    changed_data: Iterable[Belief | Goal] | Belief | Goal
    context: Iterable[Belief | Goal] | Belief | Goal

    @pl(change, changed_data, context)
    def foo(self,src, *changed_data.values, *context.values):
```

Resuming the the driver example, you can implement a plan the following way:

```py
from maspy import *

class Driver(Agent):
    def __init__(self, agent_name=None):
        super().__init__(agent_name)
        self.add(Belief("budget",(rnd.randint(6,10),rnd.randint(12,20)),adds_event=False))
        self.add(Goal("park"))

    # This plan will be executed whenever the agent gains the goal "checkPrice"
    # Every plan needs at least self and src, plus the arguments from the trigger and context
    # for this plan, the context is the belief of a budget with wanted and max prices
    @pl(gain,Goal("checkPrice", Any),Belief("budget",(Any, Any)))
    def check_price(self, src, given_price, budget):

driver = Driver("Drv")
```

TITLE: Environment

MASPY also gives an abstraction to model the environment.

Here's how you create a parking lot for the manager and driver from before:

Creating an environment

```py
from maspy import *

class Park(Environment):
    def __init__(self, env_name=None):
        super().__init__(env_name)
        # This creates in the environment a percept for connected agents to perceive.
        # This specific percept does not create a event when percieved by an agent 
        self.create(Percept("spot",(1,"free"),adds_event=False))

    def park_spot(self, agt, spot_id):
        # The function get gives you percepts from the environment
        # It has various filters to make this search more precise
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",(spot_id,"free")))
        if spot:
            # This function is used to modify the arguments of an percept.
            self.change(spot,(spot_id,driver))

            # You could also remove the old and create the new
            self.create(Percept("spot",(spot_id,driver)))

    def leave_spot(self, agt):
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",("ID",driver)))
        if spot:
            self.change(spot,(spot.values[0],"free"))
```

TITLE: Managing Beliefs and Goals

Here are some info about Beliefs and Goals being created and removed.

- This function adds a goal to the agent;
- The first field represents the goal key and must always be a string;
- The second field represents arguments of the goal and will always be a tuple;
- Each argument can have any structure, with each position of the tuple representing a different one;
- The third field represents the goal source. It is "self" by default, or another agent.
- adding or removing a goal always creates an event for agent, which will try to find a applicable plan.

```py
agent = DummyAgent("Ag")
agent.add( Goal(Name, Values, Source) )
agent.rm( Goal(Name, Values, Source) )
        
agent.add( Goal("check_house", {"Area": [50,100], "Rooms": 5}, ("Seller",27) ) )
agent.add( Goal("SendInfo", ("Information",["List","of","Information",42]) ) )
agent.rm( Goal("walk", source=("trainer",2)) )
```

- This function adds a belief to the agent;
- The first an second field work exaclty the same way as the goal's
- The third field represents the belief source. It is "self" by default, another agent or an environment.
- The fourth field dictates if the adding or removing the belief will generate a new event.
- By default it does, but sometimes one does not want a group of beliefs to be considerend new events

```py
agent = DummyAgent("Ag")
agent.add( Belief(Name, Values, Source, Adds_Event) )
agent.rm( Belief(Name, Values, Source, Adds_Event) )

agent.add( Belief("Dirt", (("remaining",3),[(2,2),(3,7),(5,1)])) )
agent.rm( Belief("spot",("A",4,"free"),"Parking",False) )
agent.add( Belief("velocity",57) )
```

TITLE: Defining Plans

To define plans it is also really simple, it only needs the @pl decoration. This decoration must contain the plan change {gain, lose or test}, the data that changed {Belief(s) or Goal(s)} and optionally a context needed to be true to execute the plan {Belief(s) or Goal(s)}.

```py
    change: TypeVar('gain'|'lose'|'test')
    changed_data: Iterable[Belief | Goal] | Belief | Goal
    context: Iterable[Belief | Goal] | Belief | Goal

    @pl(change, changed_data, context)
    def foo(self,src, *changed_data.values, *context.values):
```

Resuming the the driver example, you can implement a plan the following way:

```py
from maspy import *

class Driver(Agent):
    def __init__(self, agent_name=None):
        super().__init__(agent_name)
        self.add(Belief("budget",(rnd.randint(6,10),rnd.randint(12,20)),adds_event=False))
        self.add(Goal("park"))

    # This plan will be executed whenever the agent gains the goal "checkPrice"
    # Every plan needs at least self and src, plus the arguments from the trigger and context
    # for this plan, the context is the belief of a budget with wanted and max prices
    @pl(gain,Goal("checkPrice", Any),Belief("budget",(Any, Any)))
    def check_price(self, src, given_price, budget):
        ...

driver = Driver("Drv")
```

TITLE: Environment

MASPY also gives an abstraction to model the environment.

Here's how you create a parking lot for the manager and driver from before:

Creating an environment

```py
from maspy import *

class Park(Environment):
    def __init__(self, env_name=None):
        super().__init__(env_name)
        # This creates in the environment a percept for connected agents to perceive.
        # This specific percept does not create a event when percieved by an agent 
        self.create(Percept("spot",(1,"free"),adds_event=False))

    def park_spot(self, agt, spot_id):
        # The function get gives you percepts from the environment
        # It has various filters to make this search more precise
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",(spot_id,"free")))
        if spot:
            # This function is used to modify the arguments of an percept.
            self.change(spot,(spot_id,driver))

            # You could also remove the old and create the new
            self.create(Percept("spot",(spot_id,driver)))

    def leave_spot(self, agt):
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",("ID",driver)))
        if spot:
            self.change(spot,(spot.values[0],"free"))
```

Allowing agents to interact with an environment

from maspy import *
import random as rnd
from time import sleep

class Parking(Environment):
    def __init__(self, env_name,nr_spots=10):
        super().__init__(env_name)
        self.create(Percept("sold_spots",0,"Spots",False))
        self.print(f"Starting parking with {nr_spots} spots")
        for n in range(1,nr_spots+1):
            self.create(Percept("spot",(n,"free"),"Spots",False))
    
    def park_spot(self, agt, spot_id):
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",(spot_id,"free")))
        self.print(f"Driver {agt} parking on spot({spot_id})")
        if spot:
            self.change(spot,(spot_id,[agt]))
            return True
        else:
            self.print(f"Requested spot({spot_id}) unavailable")
            return None
    
    def leave_spot(self, agt):
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",(Any,[agt])))
        if spot:
            self.print(f"Driver {agt} leaving spot({spot.values[0]})")
            self.change(spot,(spot.values[0],"free"))
        else:
            self.print(f"Driver {agt} not found in any spot")

class Manager(Agent):
    def __init__(self, agt_name=None):
        super().__init__(agt_name,read_all_mail=True)
        self.add(Belief("spotPrice",15,adds_event=False))
        self.add(Belief("minPrice",10,adds_event=False))
        self.add(Goal("broadcast_price"))
        self.print("Manager initialized")
        self.end_counter = 0
    
    @pl(gain,Goal("broadcast_price"), Belief("spotPrice",Any))

from maspy import *
from random import randint

cnd = lambda *args, **kwargs: (args, kwargs)

class Website(Environment):
    def announce_trip(self, agt, trip):
        self.print(f"Trip announced by {agt}: {trip}")
        self.create(Percept("trip",{"seller":agt,"attributes":trip}))
    
    def delist_trip(self, agt, trip):
        self.print(f"Trip delisted by {agt}: {trip['attributes']}")
        trip_perc = self.get(Percept("trip",trip))
        self.delete(trip_perc)

        self.add(Goal("announce"))
        
    @pl(gain,Goal("announce"))

        for _ in range(5):
            stops = randint(0,6)
            trip = (stops,randint(int(700/(stops+1)),1000))

            self.announce_trip(trip)

            self.delist_trip(trip)
            self.send(src,tell,Belief("travel_ticket",trip))
        else:
            self.print(f"Trip {trip['attributes']} not found for {src}")
            self.send(src,achieve,Goal("buyTrip"))
    
    @pl(gain,Goal("improve",(Any,Any)))
    def improve_trip(self, src, improve_args):

        ```python
        from maspy import *

class Storage(Environment):
    def __init__(self, env_name=None):
        super().__init__(env_name)
        self.create(Percept("item", ("name", "quantity"), adds_event=False))

    def store_item(self, item_name, quantity):
        item = self.get(Percept("item", (item_name, quantity)))
        if item:
            self.change(item, (item_name, quantity + 1))
        else:
            self.create(Percept("item", (item_name, quantity)))

    def remove_item(self, item_name, quantity):
        item = self.get(Percept("item", (item_name, quantity)))
        if item:
            if quantity == 1:
                self.delete(item)
            else:
                self.change(item, (item_name, quantity - 1))

class Supplier(Agent):
    def __init__(self, agt_name=None):
        super().__init__(agt_name)
        self.add(Goal("supply"))

    @pl(gain, Goal("supply"))
    def supply_items(self, src):
        items_to_supply = [("apples", 10), ("oranges", 5), ("bananas", 8)]
        for item_name, quantity in items_to_supply:
            self.print(f"Supplying {quantity} {item_name}")
            self.storage.store_item(item_name, quantity)

class Retailer(Agent):
    def __init__(self, agt_name=None):
        super().__init__(agt_name)
        self.add(Belief("storage", Storage(), adds_event=False))
        self.add(Goal("sell"))

    @pl(gain, Goal("sell"))
    def sell_items(self, src):
        items_to_sell = [("apples", 3), ("oranges", 2), ("bananas", 4)]
        for item_name, quantity in items_to_sell:
            if self.storage.has(Percept("item", (item_name, quantity))):
                self.print(f"Selling {quantity} {item_name}")
                self.storage.remove_item(item_name, quantity)
            else:
                self.print(f"Insufficient stock for {item_name}")

    storage = Storage()
    supplier = Supplier(storage)
    retailer = Retailer(storage)
        ```

TITLE: Environment

MASPY also gives an abstraction to model the environment.

Here's how you create a parking lot for the manager and driver from before:

Creating an environment

```py
from maspy import *

class Park(Environment):
    def __init__(self, env_name=None):
        super().__init__(env_name)
        # This creates in the environment a percept for connected agents to perceive.
        # This specific percept does not create a event when percieved by an agent 
        self.create(Percept("spot",(1,"free"),adds_event=False))

    def park_spot(self, agt, spot_id):
        # The function get gives you percepts from the environment
        # It has various filters to make this search more precise
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",(spot_id,"free")))
        if spot:
            # This function is used to modify the arguments of an percept.
            self.change(spot,(spot_id,driver))

            # You could also remove the old and create the new
            self.create(Percept("spot",(spot_id,driver)))

    def leave_spot(self, agt):
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",("ID",driver)))
        if spot:
            self.change(spot,(spot.values[0],"free"))
```

Allowing agents to interact with an environment

from maspy import *
import random as rnd
from time import sleep

class Parking(Environment):
    def __init__(self, env_name,nr_spots=10):
        super().__init__(env_name)
        self.create(Percept("sold_spots",0,"Spots",False))
        self.print(f"Starting parking with {nr_spots} spots")
        for n in range(1,nr_spots+1):
            self.create(Percept("spot",(n,"free"),"Spots",False))
    
    def park_spot(self, agt, spot_id):
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",(spot_id,"free")))
        self.print(f"Driver {agt} parking on spot({spot_id})")
        if spot:
            self.change(spot,(spot_id,[agt]))
            return True
        else:
            self.print(f"Requested spot({spot_id}) unavailable")
            return None
    
    def leave_spot(self, agt):
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",(Any,[agt])))
        if spot:
            self.print(f"Driver {agt} leaving spot({spot.values[0]})")
            self.change(spot,(spot.values[0],"free"))
        else:
            self.print(f"Driver {agt} not found in any spot")

class Manager(Agent):
    def __init__(self, agt_name=None):
        super().__init__(agt_name,read_all_mail=True)
        self.add(Belief("spotPrice",15,adds_event=False))
        self.add(Belief("minPrice",10,adds_event=False))
        self.add(Goal("broadcast_price"))
        self.print("Manager initialized")
        self.end_counter = 0
    
    @pl(gain,Goal("broadcast_price"), Belief("spotPrice",Any))

class Website(Environment):

        self.print(f"Trip delisted by {agt}: {trip['attributes']}")

            self.print(f"Creating trip: {trip}")
            self.announce_trip(trip)

            self.delist_trip(trip)
            self.send(src,tell,Belief("travel_ticket",trip))
        else:
            self.print(f"Trip {trip['attributes']} not found for {src}")
            self.send(src,achieve,Goal("buyTrip"))

Here are some info about Beliefs and Goals being created and removed.

- This function adds a goal to the agent;
- The first field represents the goal key and must always be a string;
- The second field represents arguments of the goal and will always be a tuple;
- Each argument can have any structure, with each position of the tuple representing a different one;
- The third field represents the goal source. It is "self" by default, or another agent.
- adding or removing a goal always creates an event for agent, which will try to find a applicable plan.

```py
agent.rm( Goal(Name, Values, Source) )

agent.add( Goal("SendInfo", ("Information",["List","of","Information",42]) ) )
agent.rm( Goal("walk", source=("trainer",2)) )
```

        ```python
This is a basic example of how to define a plan in MASPY. The @pl decorator is used to specify the type of change (gain, lose, or test), the data that changed (changed_data), and optionally a context needed to execute the plan (context). The function defined within the decorator will be executed whenever the specified conditions are met.

In this case, the check_price plan will be executed whenever the agent gains the goal "checkPrice" and has a belief about its budget. The function takes three arguments: src (the source of the event), given_price (the price being checked), and budget (the agent's budget). TITLE: Managing Beliefs and Goals
        ```

Here are some info about Beliefs and Goals being created and removed.

- This function adds a goal to the agent;
- The first field represents the goal key and must always be a string;
- The second field represents arguments of the goal and will always be a tuple;
- Each argument can have any structure, with each position of the tuple representing a different one;
- The third field represents the goal source. It is "self" by default, or another agent.
- adding or removing a goal always creates an event for agent, which will try to find a applicable plan.

```py
agent = DummyAgent("Ag")
agent.add( Goal(Name, Values, Source) )
agent.rm( Goal(Name, Values, Source) )
        
agent.add( Goal("check_house", {"Area": [50,100], "Rooms": 5}, ("Seller",27) ) )
agent.add( Goal("SendInfo", ("Information",["List","of","Information",42]) ) )
agent.rm( Goal("walk", source=("trainer",2)) )
```

- This function adds a belief to the agent;
- The first an second field work exaclty the same way as the goal's
- The third field represents the belief source. It is "self" by default, another agent or an environment.
- The fourth field dictates if the adding or removing the belief will generate a new event.
- By default it does, but sometimes one does not want a group of beliefs to be considerend new events

```py
agent = DummyAgent("Ag")
agent.add( Belief(Name, Values, Source, Adds_Event) )
agent.rm( Belief(Name, Values, Source, Adds_Event) )

agent.add( Belief("Dirt", (("remaining",3),[(2,2),(3,7),(5,1)])) )
agent.rm( Belief("spot",("A",4,"free"),"Parking",False) )
agent.add( Belief("velocity",57) )
```

TITLE: Defining Plans

To define plans it is also really simple, it only needs the @pl decoration. This decoration must contain the plan change {gain, lose or test}, the data that changed {Belief(s) or Goal(s)} and optionally a context needed to be true to execute the plan {Belief(s) or Goal(s)}.

```py
    change: TypeVar('gain'|'lose'|'test')
    changed_data: Iterable[Belief | Goal] | Belief | Goal
    context: Iterable[Belief | Goal] | Belief | Goal

    @pl(change, changed_data, context)
    def foo(self,src, *changed_data.values, *context.values):
```

TITLE: Environment

MASPY also gives an abstraction to model the environment.

Here's how you create a parking lot for the manager and driver from before:

Creating an environment

```py
from maspy import *

class Park(Environment):
    def __init__(self, env_name=None):
        super().__init__(env_name)
        # This creates in the environment a percept for connected agents to perceive.
        # This specific percept does not create a event when percieved by an agent 
        self.create(Percept("spot",(1,"free"),adds_event=False))

    def park_spot(self, agt, spot_id):
        # The function get gives you percepts from the environment
        # It has various filters to make this search more precise
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",(spot_id,"free")))
        if spot:
            # This function is used to modify the arguments of an percept.
            self.change(spot,(spot_id,driver))

            # You could also remove the old and create the new
            self.create(Percept("spot",(spot_id,driver)))

    def leave_spot(self, agt):
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",("ID",driver)))
        if spot:
            self.change(spot,(spot.values[0],"free"))
```

Allowing agents to interact with an environment

from maspy import *
import random as rnd
from time import sleep

class Parking(Environment):
    def __init__(self, env_name,nr_spots=10):
        super().__init__(env_name)
        self.create(Percept("sold_spots",0,"Spots",False))
        self.print(f"Starting parking with {nr_spots} spots")
        for n in range(1,nr_spots+1):
            self.create(Percept("spot",(n,"free"),"Spots",False))
    
    def park_spot(self, agt, spot_id):
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",(spot_id,"free")))
        self.print(f"Driver {agt} parking on spot({spot_id})")
        if spot:
            self.change(spot,(spot_id,[agt]))
            return True
        else:
            self.print(f"Requested spot({spot_id}) unavailable")
            return None
    
    def leave_spot(self, agt):
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",(Any,[agt])))
        if spot:
            self.print(f"Driver {agt} leaving spot({spot.values[0]})")
            self.change(spot,(spot.values[0],"free"))
        else:
            self.print(f"Driver {agt} not found in any spot")

class Manager(Agent):
    def __init__(self, agt_name=None):
        super().__init__(agt_name,read_all_mail=True)
        self.add(Belief("spotPrice",15,adds_event=False))
        self.add(Belief("minPrice",10,adds_event=False))
        self.add(Goal("broadcast_price"))
        self.print("Manager initialized")
        self.end_counter = 0
    
    @pl(gain,Goal("broadcast_price"), Belief("spotPrice",Any))

from maspy import *
from random import randint

cnd = lambda *args, **kwargs: (args, kwargs)

class Website(Environment):
    def announce_trip(self, agt, trip):
        self.print(f"Trip announced by {agt}: {trip}")
        self.create(Percept("trip",{"seller":agt,"attributes":trip}))
    
    def delist_trip(self, agt, trip):
        self.print(f"Trip delisted by {agt}: {trip['attributes']}")
        trip_perc = self.get(Percept("trip",trip))
        self.delete(trip_perc)

        self.add(Goal("announce"))
        
    @pl(gain,Goal("announce"))

        for _ in range(5):
            stops = randint(0,6)
            trip = (stops,randint(int(700/(stops+1)),1000))
            self.print(f"Creating trip: {trip}")
            self.announce_trip(trip)

            self.delist_trip(trip)
            self.send(src,tell,Belief("travel_ticket",trip))
        else:
            self.print(f"Trip {trip['attributes']} not found for {src}")
            self.send(src,achieve,Goal("buyTrip"))

    def improve_trip(self, src, improve_args):

        ```python
        self.add(Belief("budget", (rnd.randint(6, 10), rnd.randint(12, 20)), adds_event=False))
        self.add(Goal("park"))

    @pl(gain, Goal("park"))
    def ask_price(self, src):
        self.send("Manager", achieve, Goal("sendPrice"), "Parking")

    @pl(gain, Goal("checkPrice", Any), Belief("budget", (Any, Any)))
    def check_price(self, src, given_price, budget):
        if given_price <= budget[1]:
            self.send(src, achieve, Goal("park"), "Parking")
        else:
            self.send(src, achieve, Goal("findAnotherSpot"), "Parking")

class Manager(Agent):
    def __init__(self, agent_name=None):
        super().__init__(agent_name)
        self.add(Belief("spotPrice", 15, adds_event=False))
        self.add(Goal("broadcast_price"))

    @pl(gain, Goal("broadcast_price"))
    def broadcast_price(self, src):
        self.send("Driver", achieve, Goal("checkPrice", self.get(Belief("spotPrice")).values[0]), "Parking")

class Parking(Environment):
    def __init__(self, env_name, nr_spots=10):
        super().__init__(env_name)
        self.create(Percept("sold_spots", 0, "Spots", False))
        for n in range(1, nr_spots + 1):
            self.create(Percept("spot", (n, "free"), "Spots", False))

    def park_spot(self, agt, spot_id):
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot", (spot_id, "free")))
        if spot:
            self.change(spot, (spot_id, [agt]))
            return True
        else:
            return False

    def leave_spot(self, agt):
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot", (Any, [agt])))
        if spot:
            self.change(spot, (spot.values[0], "free"))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    park_ch = Channel("Parking")
    manager = Manager()
    driver = Driver("Drv")
    parking_lot = Parking("Lot")

    Admin().connect_to([manager, driver], park_ch)
    Admin().connect_to([parking_lot, manager], park_ch)
    Admin().connect_to([parking_lot, driver], park_ch)

    Admin().start_system()
        ```

Here are some info about Beliefs and Goals being created and removed.

- This function adds a goal to the agent;
- The first field represents the goal key and must always be a string;
- The second field represents arguments of the goal and will always be a tuple;
- Each argument can have any structure, with each position of the tuple representing a different one;
- The third field represents the goal source. It is "self" by default, or another agent.
- adding or removing a goal always creates an event for agent, which will try to find a applicable plan.

```py
agent = DummyAgent("Ag")
agent.add( Goal(Name, Values, Source) )
agent.rm( Goal(Name, Values, Source) )
        
agent.add( Goal("check_house", {"Area": [50,100], "Rooms": 5}, ("Seller",27) ) )
agent.add( Goal("SendInfo", ("Information",["List","of","Information",42]) ) )
agent.rm( Goal("walk", source=("trainer",2)) )
```

- This function adds a belief to the agent;
- The first an second field work exaclty the same way as the goal's
- The third field represents the belief source. It is "self" by default, another agent or an environment.
- The fourth field dictates if the adding or removing the belief will generate a new event.
- By default it does, but sometimes one does not want a group of beliefs to be considerend new events

```py
agent = DummyAgent("Ag")
agent.add( Belief(Name, Values, Source, Adds_Event) )
agent.rm( Belief(Name, Values, Source, Adds_Event) )

agent.add( Belief("Dirt", (("remaining",3),[(2,2),(3,7),(5,1)])) )

agent.add( Belief("velocity",57) )
```

TITLE: Environment

MASPY also gives an abstraction to model the environment.

Here's how you create a parking lot for the manager and driver from before:

Creating an environment

```py
from maspy import *

class Park(Environment):
    def __init__(self, env_name=None):
        super().__init__(env_name)
        # This creates in the environment a percept for connected agents to perceive.
        # This specific percept does not create a event when percieved by an agent 
        self.create(Percept("spot",(1,"free"),adds_event=False))

    def park_spot(self, agt, spot_id):
        # The function get gives you percepts from the environment
        # It has various filters to make this search more precise
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",(spot_id,"free")))
        if spot:
            # This function is used to modify the arguments of an percept.
            self.change(spot,(spot_id,driver))

            # You could also remove the old and create the new
            self.create(Percept("spot",(spot_id,driver)))

    def leave_spot(self, agt):
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",("ID",driver)))
        if spot:
            self.change(spot,(spot.values[0],"free"))
```

```py
from maspy import *

class Manager(Agent):
    def __init__(self, agt_name=None):
        super().__init__(agt_name,show_exec=False,show_cycle=False)
        self.add(Belief("spotPrice",rnd.randint(12,20),adds_event=False))

    @pl(gain,Goal("sendPrice"),Belief("spotPrice", Any))
    def send_price(self,src,spot_price):

        self.send(src,achieve,Goal("checkPrice",spot_price),"Parking")
```

        - Do NOT invent APIs, helpers, or shortcuts
        - Follow BDI: goals/beliefs trigger plans

        PLANS
        - Only inside Agent classes
        - Only use: @pl(gain, ...)
        - Allowed in @pl: Goal(...), Belief(...) ONLY
        - NEVER use Percept(...) in @pl
        - NO keyword arguments in @pl
        - Plan body may call Environment helper methods through self.env
        - MASPY plan decorator shape is: @pl(change, changed_data, context)
        - After gain, use one trigger term and at most one context argument
        - If more than one context condition is needed, wrap them in a list as the third argument
        - Never write a fourth positional argument in @pl(...); MASPY will ignore it and the plan signature will break
        - If a plan is intended to run repeatedly while values change over time, never hardcode those changing values in Goal(...) or Belief(...) inside the decorator
        - For changing Goal/Belief values, use wildcard matching with Any, for example Goal("collect", (Any,)) and Belief("coin_count", (Any,))
        - Any is allowed in Goal(...) and Belief(...), but never in Percept(...)

        SIGNATURE RULE (CRITICAL)
        - Plan must be: def name(self, src, ...)
        - Include ONE parameter per value contained in changed_data and context Goal/Belief terms
        - Goal values come before Belief context values
        - Goal/Belief terms with no values contribute no parameter
        - If context is a list, count values across every item in the list

        Examples:
        @pl(gain, Goal("turn_on"), Belief("light_state", ("off",)))
        → def turn_light_on(self, src, light_state)

        @pl(gain, Goal("collect", (3,)), Belief("coin", (1,)))
        → def collect_coin(self, src, target, coin_value)

        @pl(gain, Goal("patrol"), [Belief("patrol_point", (Any,)), Belief("alert_state", ("pending",))])
        → def patrol(self, src, patrol_point, alert_state)

        - NEVER mismatch parameters

        PERCEPTS
        - Environment owns percept creation and percept mutation
        - Inside Environment classes, self.create(...), self.add(...), self.rm(...), self.get(...), and self.change(...) must operate on Percept(...), not Belief(...) or Goal(...)
        - Agents should not create or change Percept objects directly
        - NEVER use Any
        - NEVER use values_tuple=
        - Do NOT use True or False inside Percept values; encode state with strings instead

        BELIEFS
        - Use add(...) and rm(...)
        - Remove old belief before adding updated one
        - Use Python variables for counters
        - If a looping goal still has work left, re-add that goal
        - If the current looping goal has finished, stop, terminate, or return without calling self.rm(Goal(...)) on the same trigger goal
        - If the task says to stop or terminate after success, the success branch must explicitly call self.stop_cycle()
        - Do NOT rely on a goal being cleared automatically to stop the program
        - Do NOT use True or False inside Belief or Goal values; encode state with strings like "pending", "sent", "locked", "unlocked", "detected"

        FORBIDDEN
        get_percepts, self.get_percepts, update_belief, get_belief,
        set_belief, delete_belief, has_belief,
        self.world, world., Admin().get_environment(),
        self.update(, self.delete(, delete(,
        wait(, sleep(, log(, self.change(

        RUNTIME
        - Exactly ONE Environment class
        - At least ONE Agent class
        - Instantiate the environment in the entrypoint
        - Instantiate each agent with the environment and store it as self.env
        - Use Admin().connect_to([...agents...], env) before Admin().start_system()

        CONSTRAINTS
        - Do NOT scan percepts unless shown in CONTEXT
        - Do NOT invent environment APIs
        - Use ONLY demonstrated patterns
        - Keep implementation minimal and correct
        - NO extra features, logging, randomness

        IMPLEMENTATION PATTERN
        - Put task state owned by the world into the Environment as plain Python attributes.
        - Expose one small Environment method for each state-changing action the agent must perform.
        - Let plans trigger from Goal(...) and Belief(...) updates, not percept decorators.
        - For counter tasks: read the current belief value, call the environment helper, rm the old belief, add the new belief, then either re-add the goal or stop.
        - Re-add a goal only when more work is still required.
        - When a plan updates a belief that appears in its decorator context, that decorator context should usually use Any rather than a hardcoded literal.
        - When the final success state is reached, call self.stop_cycle() in that success path so the run finishes before timeout.
        - Represent boolean-like state symbolically using strings, not Python booleans inside MASPY terms.

        - MASPY uses @pl(change, changed_data, context).
        - The plan function must be:
        def plan_name(self, src, <changed_data values>, <context values>)
        - If there are multiple context conditions, they must be provided inside a list in the third decorator argument.
        - Every value inside the trigger and context terms contributes one positional argument.
        - If a Goal or Belief has no values, it contributes no extra argument.

from time import sleep

class Parking(Environment):
    def __init__(self, env_name,nr_spots=10):
        super().__init__(env_name)
        self.create(Percept("sold_spots",0,"Spots",False))
        self.print(f"Starting parking with {nr_spots} spots")
        for n in range(1,nr_spots+1):
            self.create(Percept("spot",(n,"free"),"Spots",False))
    
    def park_spot(self, agt, spot_id):
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",(spot_id,"free")))
        self.print(f"Driver {agt} parking on spot({spot_id})")
        if spot:
            self.change(spot,(spot_id,[agt]))
            return True
        else:
            self.print(f"Requested spot({spot_id}) unavailable")
            return None
    
    def leave_spot(self, agt):
        spot = self.get(Percept("spot",(Any,[agt])))
        if spot:
            self.print(f"Driver {agt} leaving spot({spot.values[0]})")
            self.change(spot,(spot.values[0],"free"))
        else:
            self.print(f"Driver {agt} not found in any spot")

        ```python
class CoinWorld(Environment):

class Collector(Agent):

            self.env.change(Goal("collect"), (coin_count + 1,))
        else:
            self.stop_cycle()
        ```

        ```python
        from maspy import *

class DoorWorld(Environment):
    def __init__(self):
        self.door_state = "locked"

    def unlock_door(self):
        if self.door_state == "locked":
            self.door_state = "unlocked"
            return True
        return False

    def open_door(self):
        if self.door_state == "unlocked":
            self.door_state = "closed"
            return True
        return False

class DoorAgent(Agent):
    def __init__(self, env):
        self.env = env
        self.env.add(Belief("has_key", (True,)))
        self.env.add(Belief("door_state", ("locked",)))
        self.env.add(Goal("open_door", ()))

    @pl(gain, Goal("unlock"), Belief("door_state", ("locked",)))
    def unlock(self, src, door_state):
        if self.env.unlock_door():
            self.env.rm(Belief("door_state", ("locked",)))
            self.env.add(Belief("door_state", ("unlocked",)))

    @pl(gain, Goal("open"), Belief("door_state", ("unlocked",)))
    def open(self, src, door_state):
        if self.env.open_door():
            self.env.rm(Belief("door_state", ("unlocked",)))
            self.env.add(Belief("door_state", ("closed",)))
            self.env.rm(Goal("open_door"))
            self.stop_cycle()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    env = DoorWorld()
    agent = DoorAgent(env)
    Admin().connect_to([agent], env)
    Admin().start_system()
        ```

        ```python
class DeliveryWorld(Environment):
        ```

        ```python
        }

            "package2": "location2"
        }

    def pick_up_package(self, package_id):
        if package_id in self.package_locations["depot"]:
            self.package_locations["depot"].remove(package_id)
            return True
        return False

    def deliver_package(self, package_id, location):
        ```

        ```python
class MarketEnv(Environment):

            self.stock[item] -= 1

class Buyer(Agent):
    def __init__(self, env):
        super().__init__(env)
        self.add(Belief("budget", (10,)))
        self.add(Goal("buy_item", ("apple",)))

    @pl(gain, Goal("buy_item", ("apple",)), Belief("budget", (Any,)))
    def request_item(self, src, budget):

            self.env.create(Percept("request", ("apple",)))
        else:

    def check_price(self, src, item, price):

    @pl(gain, Percept("sale", ("apple",)))
    def complete_purchase(self, src, item):
        ```

        - For counter tasks: read the current belief value, call the environment helper, rm the old belief, add the new belief, then either re-add the goal or stop.

        - Represent boolean-like state symbolically using strings, not Python booleans inside MASPY terms.

        - MASPY uses @pl(change, changed_data, context).
        - The plan function must be:
        def plan_name(self, src, <changed_data values>, <context values>)
        - If there are multiple context conditions, they must be provided inside a list in the third decorator argument.
        - Every value inside the trigger and context terms contributes one positional argument.
        - If a Goal or Belief has no values, it contributes no extra argument.

- Target: collect 3 coins
- Initial belief: Belief("coin_count", (0,))
- Initial goal: Goal("collect", (3,))

- After collecting a coin:

- the old coin_count belief is removed
- the new coin_count belief is added
